2605.13663.title: Fine-tuning with Hierarchical Prompting for Robust Propaganda Classification Across Annotation Schemas
2605.13663.paper_summary: Propaganda detection in social media is challenging due to noisy, short texts and low annotation agreements. We introduce a new intent-focused taxonomy of propaganda techniques and compare it against an established, higher-agreement schema. Along three dimensions (model portfolio, schema effects, and prompting strategy) we evaluate the taxonomies as a classification task with the help of four language models (GPT-4.1-nano, Phi-4 14B, Qwen2.5-14B, Qwen3-14B). Our results show that fine-tuning is essential, since it transforms weak zero-shot baselines into competitive systems and reveals methodological differences that are hidden using base models. Across schemas, the Qwen models achieve the strongest overall performance, and Phi-4 14B consistently outperforms GPT-4.1-nano. Our hierarchical prompting method (HiPP), which predicts fine-grained techniques before aggregating them, is especially beneficial after fine-tuning and on the more ambiguous, low-agreement taxonomy, while remaining competitive on the simpler schema. The HQP dataset, annotated with the new intent-based labels, provides a richer lens on propaganda's strategic goals and a challenging benchmark for future work on robust, real-world detection.
2605.13663.paper_summary_zh: 社交媒體中的宣傳檢測具有挑戰性，因為文本嘈雜、短小且標註一致性低。我們提出了一種新的以意圖為中心的宣傳技術分類法，並將其與一個已建立的、高一致性的框架進行比較。在三個維度上（模型組合、框架效應和提示策略），我們利用四個語言模型（GPT-4.1-nano、Phi-4 14B、Qwen2.5-14B、Qwen3-14B）將這些分類法評估為分類任務。我們的結果顯示，微調是至關重要的，因為它將弱的零樣本基準轉變為具有競爭力的系統，並揭示了使用基模型時隱藏的方法論差異。在各個框架中，Qwen模型達到了最強的整體性能，而Phi-4 14B始終優於GPT-4.1-nano。我們的分層提示方法（HiPP），在聚合之前預測細粒度技術，尤其在微調後和在更模糊、低一致性的分類法上特別有利，同時在較簡單的框架上保持競爭力。HQP數據集以新的基於意圖的標籤進行標註，為宣傳的戰略目標提供了更豐富的視角，並為未來的穩健、現實世界檢測工作提供了一個具有挑戰性的基準。
2605.13663.author: Lukas Stähelin et.al.
2605.13663.authors: Lukas Stähelin, Veronika Solopova, Max Upravitelev, David Kaplan, Ariana Sahitaj, Premtim Sahitaj, Charlott Jakob, Sebastian Möller, Vera Schmitt
